title: artemisia commutata var. scouleriana
intro: Artemisia commutata var. scouleriana, also known as Scouler's sagewort, is a perennial plant native to western North America, characterized by its gray-green foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Scouler's sagewort include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, while also possessing antioxidant properties. Artemisia commutata var. scouleriana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
